title: Vibrant Garlicky Vietnamese Shrimp Cha Gio
description: A light and garlicky Vietnamese dish featuring shrimp and dried oregano, perfect for a crowd, this recipe is a low-calorie-friendly version of traditional cha gio.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine shrimp, garlic, and dried oregano, and mix well.
Lay a rice paper wrapper on a flat surface and brush with water.
Place about 1 tablespoon of the shrimp mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the cha gio to the skillet and fry until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Remove the cha gio from the oil and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the cha gio hot with a dipping sauce made from fish sauce, lime juice, salt, and black pepper.
